prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response: In the time of harvest, the scribes of the Stock-As-Service kingdom brought forth parchments bearing tidings of a third quartus, boasting of a plentiful bounty of "recurring revenus," a mystical income that floweth steadily like the great river. Verily, the chieftain of this odd clan, called CEO, spoke of "churn rates" and " customer lifetime valuerum," as if they were magical incantations to ward off evil spirits of bankruptcius. Behold, the painted pictures of growth showed lines ascending like the heavenly stairs to Paradisum, yet my quill trembles, for I know not how a kingdom can grow without fields nor serfs. In nomine "technologia" et "scalabilitis," they claim miracles of "user acquisitionem" that put the apostles' fishnet feats to shame, and I am left to record, yet comprehend not.
